problem: Traditional ultrasound machines are expensive, bulky, and require specialized training, limiting access to cardiac imaging in point-of-care settings, rural areas, and resource-limited environments. Cardiologists and clinicians need portable, affordable imaging solutions for bedside cardiac assessments.
solution: Butterfly Network provides handheld, whole-body ultrasound probes (iQ3, iQ+) that integrate with smartphones and tablets. Their proprietary chip-based technology combined with AI-powered workflow software and a comprehensive education suite enables clinicians to perform cardiac ultrasound assessments anywhere. The platform serves individual practitioners, health systems, and medical education institutions with 145,000+ customers worldwide.
